page: Microsoft Azure D32pls_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine instance belonging to the DPLSv5 server family. It is powered by an Ampere Altra ARMv8 processor featuring the Neoverse-N1 microarchitecture. The instance provides 32 dedicated physical cores operating at a base frequency of 3.0 GHz, with one thread per core. Memory allocation totals 64.0 GB, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This configuration does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, though it is capable of utilizing Premium Storage.

Performance benchmarks place the D32pls_v5 in the average tier for most multi-core and memory operations. The instance achieved a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 57,267.23 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 12,640.0. Single-core performance is relatively weaker, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1,100.0 and a bzip3 single-threaded decompression rate of 8.74 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth is measured at 170.47 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads and 77.32 GB/sec for writes. In database and application-level tests, the server processed 41.79 million Redis SET operations per second and achieved a static web serving throughput of 32.54 GB/sec. Large language model inference speeds are poor for larger models, dropping to 2.46 tokens per second for text generation with a 70B parameter model, but remain average for smaller 135M parameter models.

The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio positions this instance as a cost-efficient option for compute-bound workloads that do not require large memory footprints. The dedicated physical cores eliminate hyper-threading resource contention, which benefits predictable multi-threaded execution. However, the lack of local storage and the absence of advanced x86 instruction sets like AVX or AMX represent notable tradeoffs for vector-heavy calculations. This instance is suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, static web serving, and lightweight database operations.
description: Microsoft Azure D32pls_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine powered by a 32-core Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 processor running at 3.0 GHz. It features 64.0 GB of RAM, establishing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio designed for compute-focused workloads with lower memory requirements. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but supports Premium Storage. Benchmarks indicate average multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 57,267.23 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 12,640.0, alongside a static web serving throughput of 32.54 GB/sec. Single-core performance is a relative tradeoff, scoring 1,100.0 in Geekbench. This instance is suited for multi-threaded tasks, software compilation, and web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32pls_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated Ampere Altra cores, 64.0 GB of RAM, and average multi-core performance suited for web serving and software compilation.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32pls_v5 is a 32-core ARM64 virtual machine with 64.0 GB of RAM, optimized for compute-focused multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-focused instance featuring thirty-two dedicated physical cores and a low-memory footprint for efficient multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 32-core Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor at 3.0 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators, with Premium Storage support
Average multi-core performance with 12,640.0 Geekbench multi-core score
Suited for software compilation, static web serving, and multi-threaded applications
